title: Set TERM inside Working Containers (by default).
body:
Feature Request
---------------------------------------------------

**Description**

TERM is not set inside buildah working containers. For me, this leads to the inconvenience that I don't get colour from my prompt - sounds trivial, but for very long make builds of, say, c++, this provides an invaluable timesaver in scrolling up to find the one root error that caused the 589 errors that occurred below because of it. Also, using fish as my shell, a surprising amount of feedback is lost without colours, I have now discovered due to this.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `set c (buildah from alpine)`
2. `buildah run $c -- ls -lah --color /`

**Describe the results you received:**
3. Output should be monochromatic.
4. `buildah run $c fish -c 'set --show TERM'` indicates that the variable is not set in any scope
5. I've also come across a few other funky errors before. We can't clear also, and sometimes curses-type stuff in the shell goes awry and I can't see what I'm doing properly.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Colours would be nice :)
This can be fixed simply by appending the environment variable to the run command. I'm guessing it's missed off the variables that are carried through automatically, whilst in `podman run`, it shows up on the inside by default.


**Output of `apt list buildah`:**

```terminal
Listando... Hecho
buildah/desconocido,now 100:1.22.3-1 amd64 [instalado]
buildah/desconocido 100:1.22.3-1 arm64
buildah/desconocido 100:1.22.3-1 armhf
buildah/desconocido 100:1.22.3-1 s390x

```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```terminal
Version:         1.22.3
Go Version:      go1.16.6
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.15.2
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```



**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=LinuxMint
DISTRIB_RELEASE=20.1
DISTRIB_CODENAME=ulyssa
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Linux Mint 20.1 Ulyssa"
NAME="Linux Mint"
VERSION="20.1 (Ulyssa)"
PRETTY_NAME="Linux Mint 20.1"
...
VERSION_CODENAME=ulyssa
UBUNTU_CODENAME=focal

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux Temp2 5.11.0-22-generic #23~20.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jun 17 12:51:00 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
Assuming not relevant; didn't even know this file existed.
```

comments.body: @stellarpower thanks for doing the analysis on this one. Just to verify do things work out for you if you add `ENV TERM=xterm` to your containerfile ? If yes then this change should be easy to add.

Relaying comment from PR so it stays with issue. 

I am not sure but this might be a potential `WONTFIX` IMO  reason mentioned in the link below

https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3539#issuecomment-927166278

Am on my phone right now, so was going to look properly layer, but was wondering if the issue should be renamed, or we should open another one and merge this into it, more in line with the PR - e.g.
 "Should (some) environment variables from defaults in containers.conf be passed into buildah run?"
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
